title: Tsomgo Lake
text: A glacial lake located 40 km from Gangtok, famous for scenic beauty and yak rides.

title: Rumtek Monastery
text: One of the largest Buddhist monasteries in Sikkim, serving as the seat of the Karmapa.

title: Yumthang Valley
text: Known as the Valley of Flowers, famous for rhododendrons and natural beauty.

title: Tashi View Point
text: Offers panoramic views of Mount Kanchenjunga and surrounding landscapes.

title: Gangtok Ropeway
text: A scenic cable car ride offering stunning views of Gangtok city and hills.

title: Namgyal Institute of Tibetology
text: A research institute and museum dedicated to Tibetan culture and history.

title: Phodong Monastery
text: A historic Buddhist monastery known for its religious festivals and architecture.

title: Ravangla Buddha Park
text: A serene park with a 130-foot Buddha statue and beautiful surrounding gardens.

title: Bara Pathar
text: A viewpoint near Gangtok offering panoramic views of valleys and mountains.

title: Seven Sisters Waterfalls
text: A group of cascading waterfalls near Pelling, surrounded by lush greenery.

title: Khecheopalri Lake
text: A sacred lake near Pelling believed to grant wishes and surrounded by dense forest.

title: Pemayangtse Monastery
text: One of the oldest monasteries in Sikkim, known for its murals and religious significance.

title: Zuluk
text: A small village on the old Silk Route, famous for scenic winding roads and sunrise views.

title: Old Silk Route
text: Historic trade route offering panoramic Himalayan views and trekking experiences.

title: Nathula Pass
text: A mountain pass on the India-China border, part of the historic Silk Route.

title: Lingdum Monastery
text: Also called Ranka Monastery, known for its peaceful environment and annual festivals.

title: Hanuman Tok
text: A small temple dedicated to Hanuman with panoramic views of Gangtok.

title: MG Marg Gangtok
text: The main shopping street in Gangtok, popular for restaurants, shops, and cafes.

title: Ranka Monastery
text: Known for its serene surroundings and spiritual significance, also called Lingdum Monastery.

title: Banjhakri Falls
text: A waterfall surrounded by a park that showcases Sikkimese folklore and culture.

title: Ganesh Tok
text: A temple dedicated to Lord Ganesha, offering views of Gangtok city.

title: Himalayan Zoological Park
text: A conservation park in Gangtok featuring Himalayan species like red pandas.

title: Tashiding Monastery
text: An important Buddhist monastery in West Sikkim, known for rituals and festivals.

title: Pemayangtse Gardens
text: Beautiful gardens near Pemayangtse Monastery with mountain views.

title: Ralong Monastery
text: A monastery in South Sikkim with traditional architecture and local festivals.

title: Sanga Choeling Monastery
text: One of the oldest monasteries in Sikkim, located near Pelling, accessible via steep climb.

title: Barsey Rhododendron Sanctuary
text: Famous for rhododendron blooms in spring, trekking and wildlife spotting.

title: Samdruptse Hill
text: A hill with a 118-foot statue of Guru Padmasambhava near Namchi.

title: Namchi Char Dham
text: Religious complex in Namchi with replicas of four Dhams and cultural displays.

title: Kunzum Pass
text: Mountain pass famous for adventure trips and Himalayan views.

title: Singshore Bridge
text: One of the highest suspension bridges in Sikkim, located near Pelling.

title: Rimbi Waterfalls
text: A scenic waterfall in West Sikkim, surrounded by forests.

title: Khecheopalri Sacred Forest
text: A holy forest near Khecheopalri Lake, protected for its sacred significance.

title: Lachung
text: A picturesque village in North Sikkim, base for Yumthang Valley and trekking.

title: Lachen
text: Village in North Sikkim, gateway to Gurudongmar Lake.

title: Gurudongmar Lake
text: One of the highest lakes in the world, located in North Sikkim, sacred and scenic.

title: Changey Waterfalls
text: Beautiful waterfall en route to Gurudongmar Lake, surrounded by Himalayan forest.

title: Phensang Monastery
text: Buddhist monastery in East Sikkim with cultural and religious significance.

title: Ravangla View Point
text: Scenic viewpoint near Ravangla offering views of mountains and valleys.

title: Singtam
text: Small town in East Sikkim, located near Gangtok and scenic landscapes.

title: Pelling
text: A small town in West Sikkim, gateway to monasteries and natural attractions.

title: Rabdentse Ruins
text: Historic ruins of the former capital of Sikkim, near Pelling.

title: Khangchendzonga National Park
text: UNESCO World Heritage site in North Sikkim, known for biodiversity and trekking.

title: Lachung Monastery
text: Monastery in Lachung village, known for spiritual significance and festivals.

title: Changey Monastery
text: Buddhist monastery in North Sikkim, popular among trekkers and visitors.

title: Temi Tea Garden
text: The only tea estate in Sikkim, offering scenic views and tea tours.

title: Barsey Rhododendron Sanctuary Trek
text: Popular trekking route during rhododendron bloom in spring.

title: Sikkim Himalayan Haat
text: Local market in Gangtok showcasing handicrafts and regional produce.

title: Lingzya View Point
text: A viewpoint near Pelling offering scenic views of mountains and valleys.

title: Martam Monastery
text: Buddhist monastery in South Sikkim known for peaceful surroundings.
